title: teucrium scordium subsp. glabrescens
intro: Teucrium scordium subsp. glabrescens, also known as a subspecies of the Wood Germander plant, is a type of flowering plant in the mint family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects, which can be attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids and terpenoids. The health benefits of Teucrium scordium subsp. glabrescens include its potential to aid in reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting the immune system. Teucrium scordium subsp. glabrescens can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
